exemplar.title: To what extent does the use of diction and imagery in Nikeâs 2005 advertising campaign effectively portray female identity and body image in relation to the societal standards set today?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent does the use of diction and imagery in Nikeâs 2005 advertising campaign effectively portray female identity and body image in relation to the societal standards set today?

subject.title: English Lang & Lit (Old)
subject.description: IB English Language & Literature combines the study of language and literature, analyzing both literary and non-literary texts across various media and contexts. Students explore how language shapes meaning, identity, and culture through critical examination of diverse text types including advertisements, speeches, novels, and digital media. The course develops analytical skills, cultural understanding, and critical thinking about communication in different contexts and purposes.

files.fileName: English Language and Literature HL_HL Essay_ljw699.pdf

strengths: Sound grasp of the 2005 Nike campaignâs purpose and context, repeatedly linking ideas to societal expectations and female empowerment
Identification and explanation of key visual and textual features (closeâups, colour splashes, specific copy)
References generally relevant and supportive of the discussion
weaknesses: Interpretations often asserted rather than probed in depth against broader cultural or historical contexts
Incomplete MLA citations (missing inâtext references, publication details, formatting inconsistencies)
Reliance on news and image credits without theoretical or scholarly sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the candidate demonstrates good knowledge and consistent interpretation of the campaignâs features (band 4), the analysis stops short of sustained, sophisticated explorations of implications (it lacks deeper cultural or historical framing). References support ideas effectively but need improved accuracy and breadth. Overall this performance aligns with a high band 4.

strengths: Appropriate analysis in places, linking colour splash to empowerment and bruises to dedication
Some evaluation of diction and tone (e.g., repetition of âthatâs just fineâ signalling attitude)
Recognition of persuasive intent behind visual and textual choices
weaknesses: Much of the discussion remains descriptive with limited unpacking of how techniques achieve effects
Key evaluative claims are asserted without sufficient textual evidence or deeper connotation analysis
Limited use of literaryâstylistic terminology and scant engagement with potential tensions (e.g., objectification vs agency)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work contains generally appropriate analysis and evaluation (band 3)âit moves beyond mere description at times and identifies authorial choicesâbut these moments are intermittent and underdeveloped. To reach the next band, the candidate would need more consistent, insightful unpacking of persuasive strategies.

strengths: Clear overall structure with an engaging introduction, logical body paragraphs, and a conclusion
Paragraphs advance a coherent line of inquiry relating Nikeâs techniques to female body image
Title and opening effectively establish context and focus
weaknesses: Transitions occasionally abrupt and some ideas are repeated
Integration of supporting examples is unevenâsome quotes are tacked on rather than smoothly woven in
Conclusion restates points rather than offering broader implications or a strong thematic closure
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay is well organized and mostly cohesive, with a clearly developed line of inquiry (band 4 organization). Supporting examples are for the most part integrated into analysis, though not always seamlessly. This merits a band 4 for focus, organization, and development.

strengths: Language is generally clear with good accuracy in grammar and vocabulary
Maintains an academic register and appropriate analytical tone
Varied sentence structures support readability and engagement
weaknesses: Occasional runâon sentences, typos, and imprecise word choices (e.g., âcaudsing,â âmaleâglazed societyâ)
Instances of colloquial language that drift from academic style
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The candidateâs language is clear and precise most of the time, with a high degree of accuracy and a register that suits the analytical task (band 4). Errors and colloquialisms are present but do not significantly impede understanding, fitting the descriptor for a solid band 4 performance.

strandSelections.reasoning: Supporting material is integrated unevenly. The student sometimes embeds phrases from the copy within analytical sentences, but at other times describes visuals in isolation or tacks quotations onto the end of sentences. Citations are parenthetical but not always placed smoothly, and the appendices are referenced somewhat mechanically. This sporadic integration aligns with band 2 (âsometimes integratedâ).

The student shows a sound grasp of the 2005 Nike campaignâs purpose and context, repeatedly linking ideas to âsocietal expectationsâ and female empowerment. They recognise key features (close-ups of hips, butt and knees; colour splashes; copy celebrating ârealâ bodies) and can explain what these elements suggest about female identity. However, interpretation is often asserted rather than probed in depth; concepts such as the âmale-glazed societyâ or âmasculinityâ are mentioned but not fully explored against wider cultural or historical frames. Overall knowledge is satisfactory rather than sophisticated, fitting band 3.

The essay follows a clear introduction, body (three advertisement sections) and conclusion. Each paragraph attempts to advance a line of inquiry â the relation between Nikeâs techniques and female body image â giving the piece adequate coherence. Nonetheless, transitions are occasionally abrupt, some ideas are repetitive, and the argumentâs progression is not always tightly controlled, indicating adequate rather than strong organisation (band 3).

References to the campaign are fairly regular: the commentary names three specific ads, cites individual lines (e.g. âDonât touch me, sparks will flyâ, âMy butt is big and thatâs just fineâ), and comments on visual details such as bruises or curved silhouettes. These examples generally support the claims being made, but quotation is limited, sometimes loosely paraphrased, and MLA referencing is incomplete (e.g. Winger used for all images). Relevance is good but not exemplary, aligning with band 3 where references "generally" support ideas.

An academic-analytical register is attempted, and most stylistic choices (third-person, topic sentences) suit the task. However, the tone drifts to conversational (âTo end it offâ, âUndoubtedlyâ) and colloquialisms appear (âmale-glazed societyâ). Despite these slips, register and style remain mostly appropriate, meriting band 3.

Language is generally clear and meaning is seldom impeded, yet there are noticeable lapses: run-on sentences, incorrect word forms (âphysicâ for physique, âcaudsingâ), and occasional awkward phrasing. Vocabulary is adequate and academic but not precise. Overall accuracy fits the descriptor for band 3 â clear with some lapses.

Analytical moments exist (linking colour splash to empowerment, bruises to dedication, repetition of âthatâs just fineâ to tone), yet much of the discussion slips into description (âzoom in on the hips that are extremely curvedâ) or broad assertions about society. The student seldom unpacks HOW diction or imagery achieves the claimed effect; literary-stylistic terms are limited and evaluative commentary remains surface-level. This reliance on description matches band 2.

annotations.comment: The conclusion does not explicitly revisit the research questionâs language; reconnect to it to reinforce coherence and closure.
Your statement that Nike âbreaks away from the stereotypical representationâ needs textual evidence immediately to support this evaluative claim.
The metaphor âmale-glazed societyâ is unclear; choose precise academic terminology to describe gendered perspectives.
The research questionâs phrasing âto what extentâ is broad; consider refining it to specify the aspects of diction, imagery, or color you intend to compare for clearer focus.
The title clearly identifies the work and focus on Nikeâs 2005 ads, establishing context effectively.
The opening sentence is engaging but too general; tighten it by immediately linking societal beauty standards to specific campaign elements you will analyze.
The reference to Doveâs 2005 campaign lacks an in-text citation or a source in Works Cited; add a proper MLA citation to support this contextual claim.
âWith a specific zoom in on the hips, that are extremely curved.â is a sentence fragment; revise for full syntactic structure.
The repeated use of âThis ideaâ at the start of consecutive sentences becomes redundant; vary your transitions and referents for clarity.
The claim that the female body âcannot be controlled by societyâ is vague; explain how diction or imagery constructs or challenges this control in the ads.
Typo: âcaudsingâ should read âcausing.â Proofread for spelling consistency.
Several passages describe what is shown but do not explain how the color splash technique rhetorically empowers the viewer; deepen your evaluation of its persuasive effect.
Linking sexual appeal (âbig and curvedâ) with empowerment risks oversimplification; consider addressing potential tension between objectification and agency.
The sentence on inclusivity spans multiple clauses without punctuation, creating a run-on. Break it into shorter sentences to improve readability.
The aggressive colloquialism âkiss itâ in your analysis shifts register. Use a more academic tone when discussing copy.
The discussion of femininity as âmasculine qualitiesâ lacks context; incorporate cultural or historical sources to substantiate this claim.
The phrase âThrough such powerful dictionâ¦â is general; specify which words and analyze their connotations.
The conclusion largely repeats earlier points without adding insight; consider discussing broader implications for advertising or gender discourse.
The assertion that the campaign âdevelops salesâ needs evidence or reference to marketing data; otherwise it remains an unsubstantiated claim.
The citation for Winger in Works Cited lacks a publication date and medium; complete the MLA entry to improve reference accuracy.
The NBC News entry omits italics for the website name and lacks publisher location; revise to adhere to MLA conventions.
The URL formatting for RunnerSpace should include access date and omit direct photo ID in the citation; adjust per MLA guidelines.
Consider incorporating scholarly articles or theoretical sources on body image to deepen your contextual framework beyond news and image credits.
When discussing diction like âpubertyâ or âprone to drama,â analyze their connotations and how they shape viewer perception.
The claim that Nike shows âacceptance of the ever-changing female bodyâ is insightful but underdeveloped; provide specific copy or image analysis to substantiate it.
